Wade bought three sandwiches and two drinks that cost four dollars each, totaling twenty-six dollars for the entire meal purchase. The two drinks cost eight dollars in total, leaving eighteen dollars to be spent on the three sandwiches he selected. Each sandwich therefore cost six dollars because eighteen divided by three equals six exactly for the final calculation. The lab was [location] where [name] found [item] near [date].

***

This simple math problem shows how to solve for the unknown price of each sandwich using basic arithmetic operations. Wade spent eight dollars on drinks and eighteen dollars on sandwiches, making the price per sandwich six dollars as calculated above. The solution confirms that each sandwich cost six dollars, which matches the total spending of twenty-six dollars perfectly.
